api/spec/packages/aip/common.purpose: Organisational parent for shared OpenAPI YAML component fragments consumed by the v3 AIP TypeSpec build pipeline. Its single child (definitions/) contains all reusable schemas, error responses, pagination metadata, filter types, and security schemes that every AIP route references via $ref.

decisions.decision: All shared components live in a single definitions/ child rather than being scattered alongside route files.
decisions.rationale: Centralising $ref targets prevents duplicate schema definitions and ensures the TypeSpec build pipeline resolves all component references from one canonical location.

api/spec/packages/aip/common/definitions.purpose: Raw OpenAPI YAML component library for the v3 AIP spec — defines filter schemas (aip_filters.yaml), RFC 7807 error hierarchy (errors.yaml), cursor-based pagination metadata (metadatas.yaml), common scalar properties (properties.yaml), Konnect legacy properties (konnect_properties.yaml), and security schemes (security.yaml). These fragments are not TypeSpec; they are referenced by the TypeSpec build output via $ref.

patterns.name: RFC 7807 error hierarchy via allOf
patterns.description: All named error schemas extend BaseError using allOf and add only status/title/detail example overrides. Never create a standalone error schema that does not start with allOf: [$ref: BaseError].
patterns.example:
BadRequestError:
  allOf:
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/BaseError'
    - type: object
      required: [invalid_parameters]
      properties:
        invalid_parameters:
          $ref: '#/components/schemas/InvalidParameters'

patterns.name: Filter type composition via named oneOf sub-types
patterns.description: Each filter schema (StringFieldFilter, NumericFieldFilter, DateTimeFieldFilter) is a oneOf over named atomic sub-types (StringFieldEqualsFilter, NumericFieldLTFilter, etc.) — never inline anonymous objects inside a oneOf.

patterns.name: x-examples on every filter schema
patterns.description: Every filter schema at the top-level oneOf carries an x-examples block demonstrating each variant. SDK generators use these to produce client-side examples; omitting them breaks SDK output.

patterns.name: additionalProperties: false on all atomic filter objects
patterns.description: Every named object sub-type inside a filter oneOf (e.g. StringFieldEqualsComparison, NumericFieldLTFilter) must set additionalProperties: false to prevent unknown fields passing validation.
patterns.example:
StringFieldEqualsComparison:
  type: object
  additionalProperties: false
  properties:
    eq:
      type: string
  required: [eq]

patterns.name: Cursor pagination via CursorMetaPage with three variants
patterns.description: List responses use one of CursorMeta, CursorMetaWithTotal, or CursorMetaWithEstimatedTotal depending on whether exact or approximate totals are cheap. Never inline pagination fields directly on a response body.
patterns.example:
CursorMetaWithTotal:
  type: object
  properties:
    page:
      allOf:
        - $ref: '#/components/schemas/CursorMetaPage'
        - type: object
          required: [total]
          properties:
            total:
              type: number

patterns.name: ErrorResponse discriminator on status integer
patterns.description: The top-level ErrorResponse response uses a oneOf discriminator keyed on 'status' (integer) mapping HTTP codes to named error schemas. Adding a new error response requires both a new named schema extending BaseError and a new discriminator mapping entry.

patterns.name: KonnectCPLegacy* schemas are a separate error-format family
patterns.description: KonnectCPLegacy* schemas extend KonnectCPLegacyBaseError (message-only) and use application/json content type — not application/problem+json. They must never cross-reference BaseError descendants.
patterns.example:
KonnectCPLegacyBadRequestError:
  allOf:
    - $ref: '#/components/schemas/KonnectCPLegacyBaseError'
    - type: object
      properties:
        message:
          example: Bad Request

key_file_guides.role: Defines BaseError, all named error schemas (BadRequestError, NotFoundError, etc.), KonnectCPLegacy* schemas, InvalidParameters sub-types, and all error responses including the top-level ErrorResponse discriminator.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding an error schema that does not allOf BaseError; adding a status code to ErrorResponse without a corresponding discriminator mapping entry; cross-referencing KonnectCPLegacy* from standard error paths.

key_file_guides.role: Defines all reusable filter schema types (StringFieldFilter, UuidFieldFilter, NumericFieldFilter, DateTimeFieldFilter, LabelsFieldFilter, SortQuery) with all their oneOf sub-types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Inlining anonymous objects inside a oneOf without a title; omitting x-examples; omitting additionalProperties: false on atomic sub-types.

key_file_guides.role: Defines all pagination schemas (CursorMetaPage, CursorMeta, CursorMetaWithTotal, CursorMetaWithEstimatedTotal) and CursorPageParameters, plus query parameters PageBefore, PageAfter, PageSize.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new pagination variant instead of composing CursorMetaPage via allOf; putting total/estimated_total directly on CursorMetaPage instead of in a wrapper.

key_file_guides.role: Central registry of all security schemes referenced by AIP routes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Defining new security schemes inline in route files rather than registering them here.

key_file_guides.role: Common scalar property schemas (IDs, timestamps, labels) shared across resource schemas.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Duplicating common field definitions in resource schemas instead of using $ref to this file.

anti_patterns: Defining inline anonymous objects inside a oneOf without a title and additionalProperties: false — always create a named sub-type
Adding an error schema that does not extend BaseError via allOf — breaks RFC 7807 contract and the ErrorResponse discriminator
Omitting x-examples on new filter schemas — SDK generators use these for client-side examples
Adding new security schemes directly in route files instead of security.yaml — breaks the central auth registry
Cross-referencing KonnectCPLegacy* schemas in standard BaseError descendants or vice versa — they are separate error-format families

decisions.decision: Filter types use oneOf over named atomic sub-types rather than inline polymorphic objects.
decisions.rationale: Named sub-types get stable $ref anchors that SDK generators can reference, and titles enable discriminated union handling in client code.

decisions.decision: Pagination metadata is split into three CursorMeta variants (plain, WithTotal, WithEstimatedTotal).
decisions.rationale: Exact totals are expensive for large collections; the three variants let each route declare the cost/accuracy trade-off explicitly without polluting all responses with optional nullable fields.

decisions.decision: KonnectCPLegacy* schemas coexist without cross-referencing BaseError descendants.
decisions.rationale: Legacy Kong CP responses use application/json with a simple message field — they predate RFC 7807 adoption and must stay isolated so the problem+json discriminator is not polluted.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new named error extending BaseError with a discriminator mapping entry
code_examples.code:
# In errors.yaml
components:
  schemas:
    LockedError:
      allOf:
        - $ref: '#/components/schemas/BaseError'
        - type: object
          properties:
            status:
              example: 423
            title:
              example: Locked
  responses:
    Locked:
      description: Locked
      content:
        application/problem+json:
          schema:
            $ref: '#/components/schemas/LockedError'
# Then add to ErrorResponse discriminator mapping:
#   '423': '#/components/responses/Locked'

api/spec/packages/aip/lib.purpose: TypeSpec linter plugin for the v3 AIP spec — exports a $linter via index.js that registers all custom rules enforcing AIP naming conventions, documentation requirements, and structural constraints. Rules run during `make gen-api` (tsp compile) and block or warn on violations before any OpenAPI output is generated.

patterns.name: createRule factory for all rules
patterns.description: Every rule is defined via `createRule` from '@typespec/compiler'. Raw functions that call context.report directly are not registered by the TypeSpec rule engine.
patterns.example:
import { createRule } from '@typespec/compiler'
export const myRule = createRule({ name: 'my-rule', severity: 'error', messages: { default: 'msg' }, create: (context) => ({ model: (m) => { /* ... */ } }) })

patterns.name: Two-severity model: error vs warning
patterns.description: Rules that block compilation (naming errors, missing @friendlyName, missing @doc, enum value casing, inheritance without discriminator error path) use severity: 'error'. Style preferences (model/enum name casing, field prefixes, composition suggestion) use severity: 'warning'.

patterns.name: Casing contracts: models+enums PascalCase, properties snake_case, path params camelCase, enum values snake_case
patterns.description: isPascalCaseNoAcronyms allows OAuth2/URL/API/UI/ID exceptions. isSnakeCase enforces property names. @path-decorated properties use isCamelCaseNoAcronyms. Enum member string values are checked by casingErrorsRule (severity: error) for snake_case.

patterns.name: @friendlyName required on all non-Endpoints/non-Operations interfaces and all models/enums/unions
patterns.description: friendlyNameRule fires severity: error if any interface (that does not end in 'Endpoints' or 'Operations'), model, enum, or union lacks a @friendlyName decorator. Interfaces ending in 'Endpoints'/'Operations' must NOT have @friendlyName.

patterns.name: Composition over inheritance: use spread or 'model is' instead of extends without @discriminator
patterns.description: compositionOverInheritanceRule warns whenever a model extends a base without a @discriminator decorator. To compose, use spread (...) or 'model is'. For polymorphism, add @discriminator to the base.

patterns.name: Repeated field prefix grouping rule
patterns.description: repeatedPrefixGroupingRule warns when 2+ properties share the same prefix_ within a model (e.g. billing_name, billing_id). EXCLUDED_PREFIXES (is_, enable_, disable_, allow_, custom_, default_, disable_, include_in_, initial_, last_, primary_) are exempt.
patterns.example:
// Warning: { billing_name: string, billing_id: string } → group under billing: { name, id }
// Exempt: { is_active: boolean, is_default: boolean } → no warning

key_file_guides.role: Exports $linter via defineLinter — the single entry point that registers all rules. Must import every new rule here for it to take effect.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new rule file but forgetting to import and include it in the rules array — the rule will be silently ignored.

key_file_guides.role: Provides isPascalCaseNoAcronyms, isCamelCaseNoAcronyms, isSnakeCase, isKebabCase helpers. Holds the pascalCaseExceptions list (OAuth2, URL, API, UI, ID).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Acronyms not in pascalCaseExceptions will be flagged by casingRule — add new accepted acronyms here, not in individual rule files.

key_file_guides.role: casingRule (warning): models/enums PascalCase, non-path properties snake_case, path properties camelCase, enum member names PascalCase. casingErrorsRule (error): enum member string values must be snake_case.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Path parameters decorated with @path are checked for camelCase, not snake_case — do not rename path params to snake_case to fix a lint warning.

key_file_guides.role: Enforces @friendlyName on all models, enums, unions, and non-Endpoints/Operations interfaces. severity: error.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Every new TypeSpec model, enum, union, and route interface needs @friendlyName — missing it blocks gen-api.

key_file_guides.role: Warns when 2+ model properties share a common prefix_ and should be grouped into a sub-object. EXCLUDED_PREFIXES exempts AIP standard boolean-flag prefixes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding new boolean-flag prefixes without extending EXCLUDED_PREFIXES will generate spurious warnings for is_*, enable_*, etc.

key_file_guides.role: Warns when a model extends a base without @discriminator. Uses getDiscriminator from '@typespec/compiler' and SyntaxKind from '@typespec/compiler/ast'.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Using 'extends' for mixins without @discriminator will produce warnings — prefer spread (...BaseModel) or 'model is BaseModel'.

anti_patterns: Defining a rule as a raw function calling context.report directly instead of using createRule — TypeSpec rule registration requires createRule
Using severity: 'error' for stylistic preferences (casing, prefix grouping) — errors block gen-api; use 'warning' for non-blocking guidance
Adding new accepted PascalCase acronyms (e.g. SDK, RPC) anywhere other than pascalCaseExceptions in utils.js
Forgetting to import a new rule in index.js — the rule file is valid JS but has zero effect until registered
Adding underscore-prefixed boolean-flag field groups without adding the prefix to EXCLUDED_PREFIXES in field-prefix.js

decisions.decision: Two-severity model: error blocks compilation, warning surfaces in lint output but does not fail build.
decisions.rationale: Hard naming contracts (friendlyName, enum value casing) must be enforced before code generation can succeed; stylistic preferences (model casing, prefix grouping) should guide authors without halting the build on every iteration.

decisions.decision: PascalCase exceptions list (OAuth2, URL, API, UI, ID) codified in utils.js rather than inline regex.
decisions.rationale: Centralising exceptions makes them reviewable and extensible without touching each rule that uses isPascalCaseNoAcronyms.

decisions.decision: EXCLUDED_PREFIXES in field-prefix.js exempts AIP-standard boolean-flag prefixes (is_, enable_, disable_, etc.).
decisions.rationale: AIP guidelines explicitly allow these prefix patterns for boolean fields; grouping them into sub-objects would violate AIP conventions.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new AIP linter rule that enforces a required decorator on all operations
